channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Study Finds Dust from California’s Drying Salton Sea Slows Children’s Lung Development
item.description: For decades, California’s Salton Sea has been shrinking due to water diversion, drought, and climate change. Once a thriving resort area, it has become a major environmental and public health problem....

item.title: Marisha Matthews Reflects on Meeting Her Partner and Their Journey Together
item.description: In 2014, Marisha Matthews was living in Adelaide with her two children when she encountered Paul, a charismatic minister, through community work supporting refugees. Their initial connection was subtl...

item.title: Victor Wanyama announces retirement after years of success in international and club football
item.description: Victor Wanyama, one of Kenya’s most decorated footballers and a former Harambee Stars captain, has officially announced his retirement from professional football. The midfielder, who started his caree...

item.title: Top football stars including Lewandowski and Osimhen to miss 2026 World Cup after qualifiers
item.description: Plenty big football names go miss the 2026 FIFA World Cup for Canada, USA and Mexico after dem fail to qualify with their countries. Poland striker Robert Lewandowski, wey don be one of the best forwa...

item.title: Economic Downturn Pushes Sugar Dating Toward Financial Survival Strategies
item.description: In Jason Parham’s WIRED article, the sugar dating world faces major shifts amid President Donald Trump's economic downturn. Many sugar babies, who traditionally depend on wealthy benefactors for finan...

item.title: Joseph Oketch named new Director-General at EPRA after board approval
item.description: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) board has officially appointed Joseph Oketch as the new Director-General of the agency. This decision comes after a competitive recruitment process...

item.title: CAN Calls On Government To Address Citizens’ Hardship and Insecurity in Niger State
item.description: For this Easter period, the Christian Association of Nigeria (CAN), Niger State chapter, don call on both the federal and state governments make dem quicken action to reduce the suffering wey citizens...

item.title: PNP reports peaceful Holy Week with nationwide safety measures in place
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) reported that Holy Week observances across the country were generally peaceful, with no major incidents recorded. Police Chief General Jose Melencio Nartatez Jr. e...

item.title: Ugandan Student Injured During Good Friday Jesus Reenactment
item.description: For Kampala, Uganda, one university student named Dennis Zziwa suffer serious injury after e dey act as Jesus during Good Friday reenactment for Our Lady of Africa Church, Mbuya. As part of the annual...

item.title: NLEX Road Warriors Secures Consecutive Wins Against Phoenix Fuel Masters
item.description: In the latest PBA Commissioner's Cup matchup, the NLEX Road Warriors successfully secured their second consecutive win by defeating the Phoenix Fuel Masters. The game saw NLEX gradually pulling away f...

item.title: Illegal Trade of Ants Threatens Kenya’s Ecosystems
item.description: A growing underground trade in ants is putting Kenya’s biodiversity under serious threat. Conservation experts warn that smugglers are capturing rare species of ants and exporting them to collectors a...

item.title: NLC orders nationwide May Day protests over non-payment of ₦70,000 minimum wage
item.description: Di Nigeria Labour Congress don show serious wahala as some states never follow di 2024 National Minimum Wage law wey President Bola Tinubu don sign. According to di law, every worker suppose dey paid ...

item.title: Josh Thomas Chats About Comedy, Chaos, and Unusual Experiences
item.description: Australian comedian Josh Thomas shares candid and often humorous insights in a recent interview, touching on a range of quirky topics. He reflects on unusual scenarios, like wanting a woolly mammoth a...

item.title: Heavy Rains Cause Dozens of Deaths and Widespread Damage in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
item.description: Since March 25, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa has been facing severe rainfall, resulting in tragic loss of life and extensive property damage. According to the Provincial Disaster Management Authority (PDMA), at...

item.title: Jean Mallard Creates Dreamlike Gouache Landscapes Inspired by Nature and Illustration
item.description: French artist Jean Mallard, born in Paris in 1997, crafts enchanting gouache paintings that transport viewers into lush, atmospheric worlds. After studying at the École des Arts Décoratifs, Mallard de...

item.title: Insights from Franchise Industry Leaders on Achieving Sustainable Growth
item.description: The Canadian franchise sector continues to expand as entrepreneurs explore scalable business models that allow for sustainable growth. Industry leaders emphasize that success in franchising requires m...

item.title: National University Defeats Adamson to Secure Second Place in UAAP Volleyball
item.description: In a decisive match during UAAP Season 88, the National University (NU) Lady Bulldogs showcased their dominance by sweeping the Adamson Lady Falcons, reinforcing their hold on the second seed in the v...

item.title: DA set for leadership vote as Steenhuisen steps down
item.description: The Democratic Alliance (DA) is gearing up for a pivotal Federal Congress on 11 and 12 April, following John Steenhuisen’s announcement that he will not seek a third term as party leader. This decisio...

item.title: WA petrol stations now required to report fuel prices amid rising global costs
item.description: Amid the ongoing global fuel crisis, Western Australian authorities are introducing new regulations that require all petrol stations to regularly report their fuel prices. This measure aims to increas...

item.title: Egypt's private sector growth slows as war raises costs and lowers demand
item.description: Egypt's non-oil private sector dey struggle for March as S&P Global PMI report show sharpest slowdown in almost two years. The PMI drop reach 48.0 from 48.9 in February, showing contraction as it rema...
